text.title: Grafana Dashboard Design Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the grafana-dashboards methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Every service dashboard MUST follow RED (Rate / Errors / Duration) for request-driven services or USE (Utilisation / Saturation / Errors) for resources.
rule.rationale: RED/USE are the literature-validated minimum panels; freeform panels miss the symptoms on-call needs first.

rule.statement: Store dashboard JSON in Git, generate via grafonnet / jsonnet where multiple dashboards share variables, and deploy via Grafana provisioning manifests.
rule.rationale: Hand-edited dashboards drift between environments; provisioning + JSON in Git keeps them reproducible.

rule.statement: Parameterise dashboards on service / namespace / region variables and forbid hard-coded label values inside panel queries.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded labels create N copies of the same dashboard; variable parameterisation collapses them to one.

rule.statement: Link every alert to the dashboard panel that triggered it and embed the alert query in that panel.
rule.rationale: Without an alert→panel link, on-call wastes minutes finding the visualisation that explains the page.

rule.statement: Match panel queries to the retention of the data source — do not query 30-day rollup for a real-time panel or 1-min raw for a quarterly view.
rule.rationale: Mismatched query range blows out backend query cost or returns no data — both kill dashboard trust.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
